message:
fix(tracking): add catch block to GA4 Measurement Protocol fetch to prevent unhandled abort errors

The GA4 MP collect request uses an AbortController with a timeout, but
the fetch had no catch block — if the request was aborted (e.g. network
timeout, DNS failure, or signal abort), the error would propagate
unhandled through the finally block. This adds explicit error logging
and sets response to undefined so the downstream status check degrades
gracefully instead of throwing.

- Widen response type to `Response | undefined` to reflect abort path
- Add catch block with console.error logging for fetch failures
- Explicitly set response = undefined on error for safe fallthrough

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
